blurb:
Chocolate Orange doesn't have a proper bio yet, so we're going to make one up based on her adorable photos. She looks like the kind of young cat who probably has opinions about everything and isn't shy about sharing them. That little face says she's got personality for days.

She appears to be a tortoiseshell or calico mix, which means she's probably got that classic tortitude. Smart, sassy, and definitely going to let you know when dinner is five minutes late. She looks young enough to still have that kitten energy but old enough to have figured out that humans are pretty useful for opening cans and providing warm laps.

Since we don't know much about her actual personality, you'll have to go meet her in person to see if she's your match. Sometimes the best adoptions happen when you just click with an animal right away. She might be the one who picks you.

DeKalb County Animal Services is in Chamblee. You can walk in during their open hours or call ahead to ask about Chocolate Orange specifically.
scoring_notes:
• Mystery cat angle creates intrigue and meet-cute potential
• Tortoiseshell personality traits appeal to experienced cat lovers
• County shelter setting adds adoption urgency

blurb:
Goober is a chocolate lab who does a spot-on Chewbacca impression. He also sings. If you're looking for a dog with actual personality quirks instead of generic "loves walks and treats," this is your guy.

He's a little cautious around loud noises and new people at first, but once you hand him a few treats, he turns into a social butterfly. He loves his rope toys, gives kisses freely, and apparently has a whole repertoire of sounds he likes to make. The Chewbacca thing alone is worth the adoption fee.

If you've been wanting a dog that's genuinely unique and a little goofy, Goober's not going to blend into the background. He's got character, and he's not shy about showing it off once he warms up to you.

Our Pals Place is in Marietta. You can submit an inquiry through Petfinder or contact them directly about meeting Goober and hearing his full vocal range in person.
scoring_notes:
• Unique Chewbacca impression creates viral potential and reader interest
• Friendly personality once warmed up appeals to patient adopters
• Chocolate lab breed popularity balances cautious nature for adoption appeal

blurb:
His name is Pork Tenderloin and yes, that's actually his name. He came from a county shelter where somebody probably thought they were being funny, but honestly, it kind of suits him. He's a handsome guy who knows what he wants and isn't shy about his preferences.

He enjoys attention, but it has to be on his terms. When he's in the mood for affection, he'll come find you and let you know. When he's not, he'd rather you respect that. He can get overwhelmed more quickly than some cats, so he does best in a calm environment where people understand that no means no and yes means yes.

Pork Tenderloin needs a cat-savvy adopter who can read feline body language and won't take his boundaries personally. If you're that person, he has the potential to form a really special bond with you. He just needs someone who gets that some cats are more particular about their social time. Good Mews is on Robinson Road in Marietta. Submit an inquiry and visit to meet him in person.
scoring_notes:
• Memorable name creates instant reader connection and shareability
• Requires experienced adopter which limits appeal but creates urgency
• Particular personality suggests he's been waiting for right match

blurb:
Eugene is the kind of cat who takes his time deciding if you're worth his attention. He's around 7 years old and has had two apartment adoptions that didn't work out. Not because he's difficult, but because he needs space to observe people from a distance before he decides you're safe.

Once he trusts you, though, he's incredible. He'll walk in big circles around you, getting closer each time until he's finally close enough for you to pet him. When he's comfortable, he purrs so loud you'll hear him across the room. He makes biscuits in your lap and his tongue sticks out when he's really relaxed and happy.

Eugene needs another cat or dog in the house for companionship and support. He likes to brush up against them and sometimes chase cats around to play or groom them. He's not great with being picked up randomly, but when he wants lap time, he'll brush against your leg and purr. That's your cue to scoop him up.

He's not ideal for apartment living. He needs room to retreat and watch from a distance. If you've got space and patience, Eugene will eventually become the most loyal companion.

To adopt, fill out an application at threepawsrescue.org. More photos at pawsumes.com/photos/27377/eugene.
scoring_notes:
• Two failed adoptions create urgency for patient forever home
• Heartwarming trust-building journey appeals to dedicated readers
• Specific housing needs limit options but reward right family

blurb:
Hazel Jordan looks like a short Nova Scotia Duck Tolling Retriever and has the most specific eating habit you'll ever see in a dog. She likes to eat laying down, taking her time with each bite instead of inhaling her food like most dogs do. It's oddly charming.

She's confident and self-assured, enjoys hanging out with people, and gets along well with selected dog friends. She loves exploring the fenced yard, going for long car rides, and taking leisurely walks. Always upbeat and happy, according to the volunteers who know her best.

If you're looking for a well-rounded companion who's got her own little personality quirks but isn't high-maintenance about it, Hazel Jordan might be perfect. She's just a solid, happy dog who happens to have table manners.

Our Pals Place is in Marietta. You can submit an inquiry through Petfinder or contact them about meeting Hazel Jordan and seeing her unique dining style for yourself.
scoring_notes:
• Perfect family dog with charming quirks appeals to wide audience
• Well-rounded personality requires minimal special accommodations
• Unique eating habit provides memorable hook for newsletter readers

blurb:
Patriot is Good Mews's mascot, which tells you everything about his personality. He's the friendliest, most outgoing cat you'll meet. He was adopted as a kitten but got hit by a car and ended up back at the shelter with some medical issues that make him hard to place. But here's the thing: he actually loves living at the shelter because he gets constant attention from everyone who walks through.

The accident left him with urine retention and fecal incontinence, which means he can't control when he goes. The shelter calls them "Patriot turds" and they're just part of life with him. He also had his tail amputated and surgery on his hip. None of this slows him down. He loves his cat friends, plays with the active ones, and if you sit down anywhere near him, he'll jump in your lap for snuggles.

Patriot needs an adopter who can handle his medical needs and won't be fazed by the occasional accident. If you can do that, you'll get one of the most loving, social cats around. He's part of Good Mews's special programs, so contact them directly to learn about adopting him.
scoring_notes:
• Shelter mascot status indicates very long wait time needing urgent attention
• Incredible backstory of resilience and joy despite medical challenges
• Special needs require dedicated adopter but extraordinary personality shines through

blurb:
Brie showed up needing a fresh start and a patient family who gets that good things take time. She's a 5-year-old orange-and-white beauty who's carrying a few extra pounds right now, but she's ready to work on that with the right feeding schedule. No free-feeding for this girl. She knows what she likes and mealtime is definitely it.

She's shy when you first meet her, but brush time is her love language. Give her a gentle head and neck brush and she melts into the happiest purrs you've ever heard. Once she's comfortable, she'll park herself next to your laptop while you work and politely request pets. She's basically the perfect work-from-home companion.

Brie gets along fine with dogs as long as they give her space, but other cats stress her out. She needs to be the only cat or maybe paired with a truly independent cat who won't bother her. She does best in quieter homes with adults or calm older teens. High energy environments overwhelm her.

To adopt Brie, fill out an application at threepawsrescue.org. Check out more photos at pawsumes.com/photos/21647/brie.
scoring_notes:
• Perfect work-from-home companion with laptop buddy appeal
• Specific care needs may limit adopter pool but shows commitment
• Sweet transformation story from shy to purring melts hearts

blurb:
Beignet has been through some stuff. She was adopted from Good Mews as a kitten, but somehow ended up scared and alone at a county shelter. Nobody knows what happened in between, but the important thing is she's back where people care about her and she's getting her confidence back.

She went straight into a foster home for some TLC and it worked. She started shy but warmed up fast once she realized she was safe. She loves being petted and is one of those cats who likes to eat with company around. When she wants attention, she'll sit close or lean against you. She can give gentle love bites when she gets overstimulated, so you'll want to know her signals and respect when she needs space.

Beignet's looking for her second chance at a home where she'll be loved and stay put this time. If you're good at reading cats and want one who's been through a rough patch but came out sweet, she might be perfect. Good Mews is a no-kill, cage-free shelter on Robinson Road in Marietta. You'll need to submit an inquiry and visit in person to meet her.
scoring_notes:
• Heartbreaking second-chance story will resonate with readers
• Sweet personality with clear recovery progress appeals to adopters
• Extended journey through multiple shelters suggests longer wait time

blurb:
Morrie showed up in late fall looking like he'd been through it. Extremely emaciated with a nasty skin infection, this little Italian Greyhound had clearly been neglected for years. But after months of TLC in foster care, he's now a healthy 18 pounds and spunky as can be. His foster mom says he's hilarious and goofy, always making her laugh throughout the day.

He's estimated to be between 9-10 years old, though it's hard to tell with dogs coming out of neglect situations. What matters is he's got personality for days and gets along great with all dogs. Despite everything he's been through, Morrie's ready to show his forever person what loyalty and love look like when a dog gets a second chance.

He'd love to find someone who wants a companion with character. If you're looking for a dog who's been through the worst and came out sweet and funny on the other side, Morrie's your guy. Email adrienne@rescuemega.org or fill out an adoption application at www.rmgdogs.org to meet him.
scoring_notes:
• Dramatic transformation from neglected to hilarious creates powerful narrative
• Senior dog with months of recovery shows dedication and urgency
• Inspiring resilience story demonstrates reward of second chances

blurb:
Someone tied Bubba and his brother Simba to a post on the playground at Austin Elementary in Dunwoody back in April. Nice harnesses, nice collars, calm and loving with everyone who approached. But nobody came back for them. Hours passed, then days, then over a week. Now they're looking for a home where they never have to worry about being abandoned again.

Bubba and Simba are bonded brothers, about 7-8 years old, yellow lab mixes who do everything side by side. If one wanders off during playtime, it's seconds before they're back together. They're house and crate trained, know basic commands, love toys and car rides and walks. They tried separating them in foster care to see if they could handle being apart, but these two are a package deal.

They need a family who wants both of them and understands that some dogs come as a pair. If you've got space for two labs who've already been through enough and just want to be spoiled rotten together, email adrienne@rescuemega.org or fill out an adoption application at www.rmgdogs.org.
scoring_notes:
• Heartbreaking abandonment story at elementary school creates emotional connection
• Bonded brothers angle appeals to readers who love loyalty stories
• Eight months waiting since April abandonment shows critical urgency

blurb:
Junior's people said they suddenly became allergic to him after years together. That was their story, anyway. Now he's at the shelter dealing with what volunteers call "black dog syndrome" — people just walk right past him without stopping to say hello.

Here's what they're missing: Junior loves ice cream runs to Bruster's and Pelican's. After his outings, he goes home with his favorite volunteer Katie, and she says he's incredible in a house. Well-mannered, housetrained, just a really good dog who knows how to live with people. He's been doing this for years.

He'd prefer to be your only dog and does better with older kids, but if you're looking for a loyal companion who already knows the ropes, Junior's your guy. He's not a project. He's just waiting for someone who won't change their mind about him.

Our Pals Place is in Marietta. You can submit an inquiry through Petfinder or call to learn about their adoption process and meet Junior in person.
scoring_notes:
• Black dog syndrome creates urgency and educates readers about adoption bias
• Perfect house companion appeals to families wanting low-maintenance pet
• Betrayal by long-term family plus ongoing overlooking suggests extended shelter stay

blurb:
Panda came to the rescue with her eight puppies as part of a court case. Based on the scars she carries, her story wasn't pretty. But all of that's behind her now. She's successfully completed treatment for heartworm disease and is officially ready for the good life she deserves.

She's about seven years old but don't tell her that. Panda still loves to get out and romp and play with dogs half her age, as long as they're big enough to keep up with her. She's not fond of small dogs, but she's great with all people and would love nothing more than a comfy sofa to curl up on for afternoon naps.

Panda's looking for someone who can show her how good life can really be. After raising eight puppies and surviving whatever came before that, she's earned some serious spoiling. If you've got a big dog at home and a soft couch, Panda would love to meet you. Email adrienne@rescuemega.org or fill out an adoption application at www.rmgdogs.org.
